title: Writing Guides

1.title: Formal Letter / Email
tips: Use a clear purpose in the opening line and keep a formal tone throughout.
Organise your message into short, focused paragraphs with linking phrases.
Include all required content points from the task and expand each one clearly.
Use appropriate openings and closings (e.g. Dear Sir/Madam, Yours faithfully).
Avoid contractions and very informal expressions.

2.title: Report
tips: Use headings and subheadings to structure the report logically.
State the objective in the introduction and summarise key findings clearly.
Support ideas with relevant examples or evidence from the task context.
Use neutral or semi-formal language and avoid storytelling style.
End with practical recommendations that answer the task directly.

3.title: Essay
tips: Address the exact question and keep your argument focused from start to end.
Use one main idea per paragraph with clear topic sentences.
Present balanced viewpoints before giving your own reasoned opinion.
Use cohesive devices naturally (however, moreover, therefore).
Write a concise conclusion that reinforces your position.

4.title: Review
tips: Introduce what you are reviewing and why it is relevant.
Describe key features and evaluate them with clear reasons.
Use engaging language but keep the structure organised and easy to follow.
Include both positive and negative points to sound objective.
Finish with a clear recommendation for a specific type of reader.

5.title: Article
tips: Use a strong title and opening to capture the reader's attention quickly.
Keep the reader in mind and use an appropriate semi-formal style.
Use paragraphs that flow logically with varied sentence structure.
Include examples, mini-anecdotes, or rhetorical questions when suitable.
Close with a final thought or call to action linked to the main topic.

6.title: Proposal
tips: State the problem and objective clearly in the introduction.
Organise ideas under clear headings such as Suggestions and Benefits.
Use persuasive but practical language focused on solutions.
Explain feasibility (time, resources, expected results) where relevant.
End with a prioritized recommendation and expected impact.

general: Plan before writing: spend a few minutes on structure and key points.
Always answer every content point in the task and stay within the word limit.
Use a wide but accurate range of vocabulary and grammar.
Leave time at the end to check clarity, cohesion, grammar and spelling.
